promptDescription: - **timearcs-gl** — WebGL-accelerated TimeArcs for large datasets. Same spec format and data semantics as `timearcs` (year/source/target/category/value, entity collapsing, force-ordered rows, year axis, activity bars, pan/zoom). Use this instead of `timearcs` whenever the result set is expected to exceed ~50k arcs (the plain SVG renderer drops frames around 10k–50k arcs and stalls into the hundreds of thousands; this renderer scales to millions). Identical SQL rules apply: lazy `"sql":"SELECT..."`, columns `year` (integer), `source`, `target`, optional `category`, optional `value`. Spec: { "type":"timearcs-gl", "title":"...", "sql":"SELECT EXTRACT(year FROM col)::int AS year, src AS source, dst AS target, type AS category, COUNT(*) AS value FROM tbl GROUP BY 1,2,3,4", "timeRange":[startInt, endInt] }. v1 limitations vs `timearcs`: no per-arc hover tooltip, no per-arc category-filter dimming, no group-toggle from arc click — only pan/zoom interacts with the arcs themselves (row-label click for group expand/collapse and legend toggles still work). If the user needs rich arc-level interaction on a small dataset, prefer plain `timearcs`.
